input_schema.description: A cat photograph to evaluate for subject clarity — whether the cat is in focus, visually distinct, and immediately recognizable as the center of the image.

A cat photograph to evaluate for focus distinction — whether the cat is rendered with perceptual sharpness relative to the rest of the image and whether its features are internally clear and recognizable.
A cat photograph to evaluate for frame presence — whether the cat occupies enough of the image to register as the dominant visual element rather than an incidental detail.
A cat photograph to evaluate for visual isolation — whether the cat is visually separable from its background and free from competing elements that might divide the viewer's attention.

description: Scores how clearly a cat serves as the subject of a photograph by evaluating three dimensions: focus distinction, frame presence, and visual isolation. A high score means the cat commands the image at first glance; a low score means the viewer must search to find it. Perceptual clarity is prioritized over technical quality.
